fact: May 26 is exactly 146 days into the year, which means there are exactly 219 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for baby rabbits to be hopping around everywhere - they're born with their eyes closed but by now they're old enough to explore!
The sun rises before 6 AM in most northern cities on this date, giving you almost 15 hours of daylight to play outside!
May 26 falls in the middle of Gemini season - people born around now are supposed to be super chatty and curious about everything!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves are turning brilliant orange and red!
Dandelions are usually going crazy right now - a single dandelion plant can produce up to 2,000 seeds that float away like tiny parachutes!
We're only about 25 days away from the summer solstice, so each day is still getting longer by about 2 minutes in northern places!
This is typically peak season for fireflies to start their magical light shows in North America - they're basically flying disco balls that blink to find their perfect match!
In many temperate places, May 26 often brings the first really hot days where asphalt gets so warm you could actually fry an egg on it!
Strawberry plants are usually pumping out their juiciest, reddest berries right around this time in northern climates - one strawberry has about 200 tiny seeds on the outside!
Mosquitoes are typically at their most annoying around this date in temperate regions because the weather is finally warm enough for them to breed like crazy in any puddle they can find!
